'''Arrakan''' was home to [[Leviathan (Arrakan)|Leviathans]].<ref name="Myth" />

==Behind the scenes==
Arrakan was first introduced in the unlicensed roleplaying adventure "[[Flight of the Jedi]]", which is considered non-canon due to a lack of [[Lucas Licensing]] approval.

===Non-canon content===
'''Arrakan''' was a [[Water/Legends|water]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Sulphis system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], on the opposite side of [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]] from [[Lennax]]. Its name meant the ''Blue Pearl.'' Arrakan's skies were filled with violent [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] storms and glowering clouds connected to the water surface by "vast whirlwind twisters of air and water."

The indigenous [[Battrach/Legends|Battrachs]] maintained a floating harbor at a coral-like island, where [[Starship/Legends|starships]] landed in moored giant bivalves, which then closed their shells and carried the craft to the underwater [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Kearleonis]]. Animals could travel underwater in giant jellyfish. The Battrachs, the bivalves, and the jellyfish were all genetically engineered by the indigenous [[Duor]]s, who themselves believed they had been made by a millennium-old [[Leviathan (Arrakan)|Leviathan]] named [[Kithnarrock]].

Kithnarrock was a contact for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. A number of survivors of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] contacted the Alliance through Kithnarrock; however, this resulted in an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] attacking Kithnarrock.
